content.content:
[1;37mJurisdiction Handbook[0m
[0mBUK reaches remote nodes through two operator-facing ceremonies.[0m
[0mCaparison establishes admin host posture per platform[0m
[0m(Windows/macOS/Linux); Garrison provisions the workload account.[0m
[0mBUK never generates or modifies SSH key material; the operator[0m
[0mowns all key administration.[0m

[0mOn Windows, caparison-windows handles first-run admin trust[0m
[0mitself: its phase-1 admin SSH session uses[0m
[0mPreferredAuthentications=publickey,password, falls through to a[0m
[0m/dev/tty password prompt on a fresh node, the operator types the[0m
[0madmin password once, caparison-windows installs the admin pubkey[0m
[0m+ sshd hardening + restarts sshd, and phase 2 reconnects by key[0m
[0malone. Subsequent caparison-windows and garrison runs use key[0m
[0mauth automatically. The operator's manual scope on Windows[0m
[0mreduces to: make sshd reachable on the network with a known[0m
[0madmin password.[0m

[0mOn Linux/Mac, admin trust is operator-manual (ssh-copy-id or[0m
[0mequivalent); caparison-{linux,macos} runs after that to apply[0m
[0mper-platform host posture. Garrison then provisions the workload[0m
[0maccount against the key-trusted admin foothold.[0m

[1;37mLinux and macOS[0m
[0msshd is typically already installed and reachable. The operator's[0m
[0mmanual scope is: confirm sshd is running and the host is reachable,[0m
[0mthen place the admin pubkey via ssh-copy-id (or equivalent) so[0m
[0mgarrison's first admin SSH succeeds by key alone. Caparison-[0m
[0m{linux,macos} then applies per-platform host posture; sshd[0m
[0mhardening on non-Windows-OpenSSH nodes is operator-managed.[0m

[36mssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/<admin-pubkey>.pub <admin-user>@<host>[0m

[1;37mWindows: first-time host setup[0m
[0mOn a Windows host that has never had caparison-windows run[0m
[0magainst it, complete the first-time host preparation[0m
[0m(optional Tailscale autonomy, OpenSSH install, sshd[0m
[0mreachability) before proceeding to caparison/garrison:[0m

[0m  [36mtt/buw-hjw.HandbookJurisdictionWindows.sh[0m[0m

[1;37mRun Caparison, then Garrison[0m
[0mOn Windows: with sshd reachable, run caparison-windows first.[0m
[0mCaparison-Windows establishes admin trust (typing the admin[0m
[0mpassword once when its phase-1 ssh prompts on /dev/tty), hardens[0m
[0msshd_config to key-only, restarts sshd, and reconnects to[0m
[0mverify. After caparison-windows succeeds, run garrison for the[0m
[0mchosen workload shell:[0m

[0m  buw-jpCW <investiture>  — caparison-windows (Windows OpenSSH only)[0m

[0m  buw-jpGb <investiture>  — native bash workload (Linux, macOS)[0m
[0m  buw-jpGc <investiture>  — Cygwin bash workload (Windows)[0m
[0m  buw-jpGw <investiture>  — WSL bash workload (Windows)[0m

[0mChoose c or w for Windows by which workload runtime you need.[0m
[0mBoth share caparison-windows's admin SSH harden; the shell-letter[0m
[0mon garrison only routes the workload account ceremony.[0m

[0mgarrison-w prerequisite: the admin user must have[0m
[0mWSL distribution rbtww-main installed (run wsl --install[0m
[0mor buw-jpW) before garrison-w runs. garrison-w exports[0m
[0madmin's rbtww-main to a seed tarball and imports that[0m
[0mtarball under the workload user's HKCU\Lxss — Microsoft's[0m
[0mper-user WSL design forces the workload to own its own[0m
[0mdistribution registration. Keep admin's rbtww-main[0m
[0mpristine; admin's customizations propagate to workload[0m
[0mvia the seed.[0m

[0mOn Linux/Mac: place the admin pubkey via ssh-copy-id (if not[0m
[0malready in place), then run caparison-{linux,macos} to apply[0m
[0mhost posture. Run garrison against the key-trusted admin[0m
[0mfoothold:[0m

[0m  buw-jpCM <investiture>  — caparison-macos[0m
[0m  buw-jpCL <investiture>  — caparison-linux[0m

[0mAfter garrison succeeds, dispatch work via the workload[0m
[0mtabtargets:[0m
[0m  buw-jwk <investiture>           — probe workload reachability[0m
[0m  buw-jwc <investiture> <file>    — run a command file[0m
[0m  buw-jws <investiture>           — interactive workload session[0m
hj0 OK
[1;32mPASSED:[0m rbtdrf_hb_payor_refresh
[1;32mPASSED:[0m rbtdrf_hb_quota_build

[1;32m15 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped (15 total)[0m

file.content:
buc_step "  [2/6] Ensure ${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root} directory"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path '${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}' -Force | Out-Null" \
    || buc_die "Failed to create ${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}"

  buc_step "  [3/6] Install ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} seed distribution"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --install --no-launch -d ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to install ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} seed"

  buc_step "  [4/6] Export ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} to ${z_tar_path}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --export ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} '${z_tar_path}'" \
    || buc_die "Failed to export ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}"

  buc_step "  [5/6] Import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} from ${z_tar_path}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} '${z_distro_dir}' '${z_tar_path}'" \
    || buc_die "Failed to import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"

  buc_step "  [6/6] Cleanup: unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} and remove .tar"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}"
  z_tar_present=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged "Test-Path '${z_tar_path}'") \
    || buc_die "Failed to probe ${z_tar_path}"
  if [[ "${z_tar_present}" == "True" ]]; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Force '${z_tar_path}'" \
      || buc_die "Failed to remove ${z_tar_path}"
  fi

  buc_step "  Stage WSL succeeded"
}

# node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER under key-only auth. Pass-through: argv is
# handed to ssh as the remote command without shell wrapping (operator

540	# zbujb_admin_powershell BODY... -- run a PowerShell statement chain on
541	# the remote node as the privileged user. Prepends discipline prelude:
542	# $ErrorActionPreference='Stop' (PS errors terminate), $env:WSL_UTF8=1
543	# (wsl.exe emits UTF-8), $LASTEXITCODE=0 (initialize so the trailing
544	# if-check doesn't trip on the $null default — $null -ne 0 evaluates True
545	# in PowerShell typed comparison, which would fire `exit $null` and
546	# discard buffered object-formatter output for cmdlets like Get-LocalUser
547	# whose tables haven't flushed to stdout yet). Trailing if-check
548	# propagates $LASTEXITCODE on non-zero from native commands in the body.
549	# Caller redirects stdout/stderr; returns ssh's exit code.
550	zbujb_admin_powershell() {
551	  zbujb_sentinel
552	  test $# -ge 1 \
553	    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_powershell: PowerShell command body required"
554	  local -r z_body="$*"
555	
556	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"        \
557	      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"             \
558	      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"      \
559	      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"  \
560	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"  \
561	      "${BUJB_ps_invoke_command} \"${BUJB_ps_prelude} ${z_body}; if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit \$LASTEXITCODE }\""
562	}
563	
564	# zbujb_powershell_capture ROLE BODY -- run a single PS expression on the
565	# remote node as the named role and emit its stdout. Windows CR is stripped
566	# via bash parameter expansion (no subprocess) so $(...) yields a clean
567	# value. Single-expression discipline per WSG-PS-5; multi-statement bodies
568	# do not belong here. Caller || buc_die on capture failure.
569	# ROLE: zbujb_privileged | zbujb_workload

file.content:
# zbujb_admin_powershell BODY... -- run a PowerShell statement chain on
# the remote node as the privileged user. Prepends discipline prelude:
# $ErrorActionPreference='Stop' (PS errors terminate), $env:WSL_UTF8=1
# (wsl.exe emits UTF-8), $LASTEXITCODE=0 (initialize so the trailing
# if-check doesn't trip on the $null default — $null -ne 0 evaluates True
# in PowerShell typed comparison, which would fire `exit $null` and
# discard buffered object-formatter output for cmdlets like Get-LocalUser
# whose tables haven't flushed to stdout yet). Trailing if-check
# propagates $LASTEXITCODE on non-zero from native commands in the body.
# Caller redirects stdout/stderr; returns ssh's exit code.
zbujb_admin_powershell() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test $# -ge 1 \
    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_powershell: PowerShell command body required"
  local -r z_body="$*"

  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"        \
      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"             \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"      \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"  \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"  \
      "${BUJB_ps_invoke_command} \"${BUJB_ps_prelude} ${z_body}; if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit \$LASTEXITCODE }\""
}

# zbujb_powershell_capture ROLE BODY -- run a single PS expression on the
# remote node as the named role and emit its stdout. Windows CR is stripped
# via bash parameter expansion (no subprocess) so $(...) yields a clean
# value. Single-expression discipline per WSG-PS-5; multi-statement bodies
# do not belong here. Caller || buc_die on capture failure.
# ROLE: zbujb_privileged | zbujb_workload

```
wsl.exe --install --no-launch -d Ubuntu-24.04
```

That command pulls the Ubuntu-24.04 distribution image (~700MB–1GB) from the Microsoft Store on the Windows host. `--no-launch` suppresses the interactive download-progress UI that wsl.exe normally renders, and PowerShell-over-SSH discards what little progress it does emit, which is why your terminal looks frozen at [3/6] with nothing streaming back.

**Diagnosis:** healthy, just waiting. Typical wait is 5–15 minutes depending on the Windows host's bandwidth. The local bash is correctly blocked on SSH; if you want a sanity check on the Windows side, you could open a separate terminal and run `tt/buw-jpS.PrivilegedSsh.sh bujn-winpc 'wsl.exe --list --verbose'` — but I'd just let it finish first since concurrent wsl.exe calls can confuse the install state machine.

content.text:
Caparison phases 1-3 succeeded (Ubuntu downloaded, exported, imported, cleanup, powercfg, Tailscale all clean). Bug is in **post-completion `invigilate-windows`** — it treats the `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion=0` registry key as a required fact:

```
Fact: registry DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion = 0
Get-ItemPropertyValue : Cannot find path '...PasswordLess\Device'...
ERROR: registry DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion: expected 0, got '<unreadable>' — operator handbook BUSJHW (Windows registry step)
```

That registry key is the **netplwiz / auto-login workaround for Microsoft accounts on Windows 11** — exactly the optional sub-step we just moved into `buw-hjw`'s availability section. It's only needed when:
1. The operator wants Windows auto-login, AND
2. The host is signed in with a Microsoft account (so the netplwiz checkbox is hidden)

Treating it as a required precondition fact is incorrect — it should be optional. Let me look at the invigilate code.

      "Get-ItemPropertyValue -Path 'HKLM:\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Power' -Name 'PlatformAoAcOverride'") \
    || z_reg="<unreadable>"
  test "${z_reg}" = "0" \

  local z_pcfg

  z_pcfg="${z_pcfg//$'\r'/}"
  local z_available_section="${z_pcfg%%not available on this system*}"
  case "${z_available_section}" in
    *Standby*|*Hibernate*)
      buc_die "powercfg available sleep states: expected neither Standby nor Hibernate listed as available (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW) — or AoAc override missing"

  case $'\n'"${z_wsl}"$'\n' in
    *$'\n'"${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"$'\n'*) ;;
    *)
      buc_die "WSL distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}: expected present in wsl.exe --list --quiet, got '${z_wsl:-<none reported>}' — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
      ;;
  esac

  buc_step "Invigilate-windows succeeded"
}

content.text:
Here's the bug — a design contradiction between the handbook and `bujb_invigilate_windows`:

**Invigilate-windows requires three operator-precondition facts** (lines 1836–1858):

| Fact | Pointer in error |
|---|---|
| `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion = 0` | "operator handbook BUSJHW (Windows registry step)" |
| `PlatformAoAcOverride = 0` | "operator handbook BUSJHW (Modern Standby override)" |
| Tailscale service registered | "operator handbook BUSJHW (install + Run-Unattended + first auth)" |

These were minted as required facts in pace ₢A-ABE (extend-busji-operator-precondition-facts).

**But the handbook framing — even before our recent move — says these are OPTIONAL.** The `Windows: Host Availability (optional)` section (now in `buw-hjw`) opens with: *"Skip this section if an operator logs the host in after every restart."* So an operator who's fine with manual logon never sets the registry key, never installs Tailscale, never gets `PlatformAoAcOverride=0` — and then `invigilate-windows` dies on the first such fact.

Your `rocket` host hit fact #2 (`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion`) because it wasn't ever set — that registry path doesn't even exist on hosts that haven't done the workaround.

**Two ways to resolve:**

**A. The facts are genuinely required.** Then the `(optional)` framing in `buw-hjw` is wrong — these are mandatory preconditions for any caparison-windows-managed host. Rename the section, drop the "Skip if…" sentence, and add it as a required preface to the sshd-reachability flow. Operator must do all of: install Tailscale, set Run-Unattended, configure auto-login (with registry workaround if Microsoft account), and probably also set `PlatformAoAcOverride=0` (which isn't even in the current handbook!).

**B. The facts are genuinely optional.** Then `invigilate-windows` is overreaching — it should either skip these checks when the registry path doesn't exist (treating absent-as-acceptable), or move them to a separate "audit unattended posture" verb that the operator runs only on hosts where they want unattended mode.

Also note: `PlatformAoAcOverride = 0` is checked but **never appears in the handbook at all** — that's a separate gap regardless of which path you choose.

Which way? My read is **B** — these facts represent a separate concern (unattended-power-on posture) from caparison's core job (admin trust + sshd harden + WSL stage + Tailscale-service-running). They're orthogonal to whether caparison can succeed on a host. But you've thought more about the design than I have.

Let me query both paths.

content.text:
Confirmed — the registry value IS set correctly to `0` at:

```
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device
```

…and the second query (the `Windows`-without-`NT` path) returned **nothing** because that path doesn't exist.

I was wrong in my prior message — I apologize for blaming the operator. The bug is unambiguously in `bujb_invigilate_windows` at line 1839: it queries `HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device`, missing the `NT`. The handbook's path is correct; invigilate's is wrong.

Want me to fix it (single-character edit — insert `NT` after `Windows` on line 1839) and re-run caparison-windows? Worth also checking whether `PlatformAoAcOverride` at line 1847 has a similar path typo before we move on.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUBC — BUK Bootstrap Constants
# Source-time literal constants for BUK kit layout.
# No kindle dependency — available immediately upon sourcing.

# Guard against multiple inclusion
test -z "${ZBUBC_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUBC_SOURCED=1

# Source-time literal constants
BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir="rbmn_nodes"
BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir="rbmu_users"

# Platform identifiers (bubep_* — bub/bube/bubep_* tree).
# These values are the canonical OS-family identifiers used in BURN_PLATFORM
# enrollment and in per-tabtarget platform invariant assertions.
# BUBC_platforms_<family> tinder constants provide single source of truth so
# code refers to the identifier by family name rather than hardcoding the
# literal token at every comparison site.
BUBC_platforms_linux="bubep_linux"
BUBC_platforms_mac="bubep_mac"
BUBC_platforms_windows="bubep_windows"

# Windows OpenSSH layout — forward slashes throughout so identical strings
# work in PowerShell, terminal display, and icacls invocations.
BUBC_windows_sshd_config='C:/ProgramData/ssh/sshd_config'
BUBC_windows_admin_auth_keys='C:/ProgramData/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys'
BUBC_windows_ssh_port="22"
BUBC_windows_fw_rule_name="sshd"
BUBC_windows_fw_display_name="OpenSSH Server"

# eof

file.content:
# bujb_caparison_windows -- run the three-phase caparison-windows ceremony
# for a Windows OpenSSH node. Phase 1: admin pubkey install + sshd_config
# harden + sshd -t + Restart-Service sshd. Phase 2: reconnect under
# key-only auth + re-verify directives. Phase 3: WSL distribution stage +
# powercfg sleep/hibernate disable + Tailscale service auto-start. Closes
# with post-completion invigilate-windows. Caller must have invoked
# bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
bujb_caparison_windows() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_caparison_windows: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
  test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
    || buc_die "bujb_caparison_windows: requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"

  buc_step "Caparison-windows: ${BUZ_FOLIO} (${BURN_HOST})"

  zbujb_caparison_windows_phase1
  zbujb_caparison_windows_phase2
  zbujb_caparison_windows_phase3

  buc_step "  Post-completion check: invigilate-windows"
  bujb_invigilate_windows

  buc_step "Caparison-windows succeeded"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: WSL stage helper (invoked by bujb_caparison_windows phase 3)

# zbujb_caparison_windows_stage_wsl -- idempotently provision
# BUJB_wsl_distribution by purging any prior state, installing an
# Ubuntu-24.04 seed, exporting it to a .tar, importing under the
# canonical name, then unregistering the seed and removing the .tar.
# Caller is bujb_caparison_windows phase 3, which has already asserted
# resolve + bubep_windows. Each step propagates failure via

BUBC_windows_fw_rule_name="sshd"
BUBC_windows_fw_display_name="OpenSSH Server"

# Windows registry preconditions for unattended power-on posture.
# Operator-handbook step (BUSJHW Windows: Host Availability) sets these;
# bujb_invigilate_windows reads them. Single source of truth so the path
# the handbook tells the operator to set is the path invigilate queries.
# PowerShell-canonical form (HKLM:\ prefix, mixed case — registry is
# case-insensitive at the OS level so display case is purely cosmetic).
BUBC_windows_passwordless_path='HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device'
BUBC_windows_passwordless_value='DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion'
BUBC_windows_aoac_path='HKLM:\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power'
BUBC_windows_aoac_value='PlatformAoAcOverride'

# eof

#

# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUBC — BUK Bootstrap Constants
# Source-time literal constants for BUK kit layout.
# No kindle dependency — available immediately upon sourcing.

# Guard against multiple inclusion
test -z "${ZBUBC_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUBC_SOURCED=1

# Source-time literal constants
BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir="rbmn_nodes"
BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir="rbmu_users"

# Platform identifiers (bubep_* — bub/bube/bubep_* tree).
# These values are the canonical OS-family identifiers used in BURN_PLATFORM
# enrollment and in per-tabtarget platform invariant assertions.
# BUBC_platforms_<family> tinder constants provide single source of truth so
# code refers to the identifier by family name rather than hardcoding the
# literal token at every comparison site.
BUBC_platforms_linux="bubep_linux"
BUBC_platforms_mac="bubep_mac"
BUBC_platforms_windows="bubep_windows"

# Windows OpenSSH layout — forward slashes throughout so identical strings
# work in PowerShell, terminal display, and icacls invocations.
BUBC_windows_sshd_config='C:/ProgramData/ssh/sshd_config'
BUBC_windows_admin_auth_keys='C:/ProgramData/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys'
BUBC_windows_ssh_port="22"
BUBC_windows_fw_rule_name="sshd"
BUBC_windows_fw_display_name="OpenSSH Server"

# eof

  test "${z_reg}" = "0" \
    || buc_die "registry DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion: expected 0, got '${z_reg:-<empty>}' — operator handbook BUSJHW (Windows registry step)"

# bujb_invigilate_windows -- BUSJIW read-only host posture verification.

input.new_string:
# zbujb_invigilate_windows_op_facts -- the three operator-handbook
# preconditions that gate caparison-windows phase 3 (the WSL stage's
# multi-minute download). Shared between bujb_caparison_windows preflight
# (early-fail before WSL) and bujb_invigilate_windows (post-completion
# audit + standalone runs). Requires admin SSH already established under
# key-only auth.
zbujb_invigilate_windows_op_facts() {
  zbujb_sentinel

  buc_step "  Fact: registry ${BUBC_windows_passwordless_value} = 0"
  local z_reg=""
  z_reg=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged \

  buc_step "  Fact: registry ${BUBC_windows_aoac_value} = 0"
  z_reg=""
  z_reg=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged \
      "Get-ItemPropertyValue -Path '${BUBC_windows_aoac_path}' -Name '${BUBC_windows_aoac_value}'") \
    || z_reg="<unreadable>"
  test "${z_reg}" = "0" \
    || buc_die "registry ${BUBC_windows_aoac_value}: expected 0, got '${z_reg:-<empty>}' — operator handbook BUSJHW (Modern Standby override)"

  buc_step "  Fact: Tailscale service registered"
  local z_svc=""
  z_svc=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged \
      "Get-Service Tailscale -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Name") \

  test -n "${z_svc}" \

}

| `Tools/buk/buhj_jurisdiction.sh` | Step 3.1 of `zbuhj_render_windows_availability` now renders the registry path/value from constants instead of hardcoded literals |
| `Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh` | New `zbujb_invigilate_windows_op_facts` (the three operator-handbook preconditions); fixed registry path bug (`Windows` → `Windows NT`) by routing through the constant; `bujb_invigilate_windows` calls the helper instead of inlining the three checks; `bujb_caparison_windows` calls it as a **preflight gate between phase 2 and phase 3**, saving the operator ~5 min of WSL download when a precondition fails |

**Two observations to flag:**

1. **Handbook gap on `PlatformAoAcOverride`** — invigilate (and now preflight) requires it, but the handbook never tells the operator to set it. This is the missing prereq you discovered the hard way. The constants are defined and ready — just need a handbook step. I left it out to keep this change scoped; want me to add it as a separate step in `zbuhj_render_windows_availability`?

2. **Live caparison run on rocket** — preflight is now in place but you'll need to re-run `tt/buw-jpCW.CaparisonWindows.sh bujn-winpc` to actually verify it lands. The earlier run died in invigilate post-completion, so caparison's destructive work (sshd harden, WSL stage) already completed. The re-run will pass preflight (registry is set), then re-do everything idempotently.

Want me to notch what's done, or wait until after we sort #1 and #2?

zbuhj_render_windows_availability() {
  buh_section  "Windows: Host Availability (optional)"
  buh_line     "Skip this section if an operator logs the host in after every restart."
  buh_line     "If the host lives in a physically-secured single-occupant room and"
  buh_line     "must come back from power loss or OS-update reboots without a console"
  buh_line     "operator, configure Tailscale unattended mode and Windows auto-login"
  buh_line     "before the sshd-reachability ceremony below."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Precondition:"
  buh_line     "- Host lives in a physically-secured, single-occupant room."
  buh_line     "  Auto-login removes the keyboard barrier; physical access becomes"
  buh_line     "  total access."
  buh_e

  buh_step1    "Install Tailscale:"
  buh_link     "" "Tailscale for Windows" "https://tailscale.com/download/windows"
  buh_line     "The installer registers a per-user auto-start; default behavior is fine."
  buh_e

  buyy_ui_yawp "Preferences"; local -r z_prefs="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_ui_yawp "Run unattended"; local -r z_unatt="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_step1    "Enable Run Unattended (tunnel survives without an interactive session):"
  buh_line     "Right-click the Tailscale tray icon, hover ${z_prefs}, select ${z_unatt}."
  buh_warn     "First boot quirk: the tunnel may require one interactive login before"
  buh_warn     "unattended mode settles (Tailscale issue #3186). Log in once, reboot,"
  buh_warn     "then verify from another node before walking away."
  buh_e

  buyy_cmd_yawp "netplwiz"; local -r z_netplwiz="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_ui_yawp "Users must enter a user name and password to use this computer"; local -r z_checkbox="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_step1    "Configure Windows Auto-Login via netplwiz:"
  buh_line     "Press Win+R, enter ${z_netplwiz}, press Enter."
  buh_line     "Select the target user. Uncheck ${z_checkbox}."
  buh_line     "Click Apply. Enter the user's password twice when prompted."
  buh_e

  buyy_cmd_yawp "regedit"; local -r z_regedit="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_step2    "If the checkbox is absent (Microsoft account on Windows 11):"
  buh_line     "Open ${z_regedit} and set:"
  buh_code     "${BUBC_windows_passwordless_path}"
  buh_code     "    ${BUBC_windows_passwordless_value} = 0   (DWORD)"
  buh_line     "Reopen netplwiz; the checkbox now appears."
}

Constants minted in bubc_constants.sh: BUBC_windows_passwordless_path/value (HKLM:\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\PasswordLess\\Device + DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion) and BUBC_windows_aoac_path/value (HKLM:\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Power + PlatformAoAcOverride). PowerShell-canonical form (HKLM:\\ prefix, mixed case — registry is case-insensitive at OS level). buhj_jurisdiction.sh's zbuhj_render_windows_availability now sources the registry path/value strings from constants for the netplwiz Microsoft-account workaround (step 3.1), and gains a new step 4 'Disable Modern Standby' that documents the AoAc override the operator previously had to discover the hard way — the override is required because powercfg standby/hibernate disable that caparison-windows applies is silently ignored on Modern Standby systems unless this is set first. Reboot guidance included. bujb_jurisdiction.sh: extracted the three operator-handbook precondition fact checks (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion, PlatformAoAcOverride, Tailscale service registered) from bujb_invigilate_windows into a new shared private helper zbujb_invigilate_windows_op_facts. The extraction simultaneously fixes the original bug — the inline DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion check in bujb_invigilate_windows queried 'HKLM:\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\PasswordLess\\Device' (missing 'NT'), which is why the registry value the operator correctly set per handbook was reported as <unreadable>; the extracted helper queries via BUBC_windows_passwordless_path which carries the correct 'Windows NT' segment. bujb_invigilate_windows now calls the helper instead of inlining the three checks (reducing duplication and ensuring path drift can't recur). bujb_caparison_windows orchestrator gains a [Preflight] step between zbujb_caparison_windows_phase2 and zbujb_caparison_windows_phase3 that calls zbujb_invigilate_windows_op_facts — when an operator-precondition fact fails, the operator is told within seconds rather than after the ~5 minute wsl.exe --install download (measured: WSL stage took 6m11s on the rocket run, dominated by the wsl.exe --install proper). Three changes in one commit:
1. **`bubc_constants.sh`** — four new tinder constants (passwordless + AoAc registry path/value pairs)
2. **`buhj_jurisdiction.sh`** — handbook 3.1 sources from constants; new step 4 documents the AoAc override (the prereq you had to discover the hard way)

content.text:
**Preflight is working perfectly** — caught the missing precondition in **seconds** instead of 6 minutes after the WSL download. That's exactly the value the just-landed change was supposed to deliver.

**The error itself isn't a bug** — it's the genuine missing precondition that the just-added step 4 in the handbook documents. PowerShell error reads:

> `Property PlatformAoAcOverride does not exist at path HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power.`

The registry **path** exists (it's a stock Windows key); the **value** doesn't. You need to add it.

Per the new handbook step 4 (`tt/buw-hjw.HandbookJurisdictionWindows.sh`):
```
HKLM:\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power
    PlatformAoAcOverride = 0   (DWORD)
```
…and **reboot** (the override only takes effect after a fresh boot, since power-state config is read at startup).

Two options for setting it:

**A. Operator does it via regedit on rocket** — exactly what the handbook prescribes; requires console access to rocket or RDP.

**B. I set it now via privileged SSH** — one-liner, keeps you in this session. Caveat: still need to reboot rocket afterward before re-running caparison-windows. Want me to issue:

```powershell
New-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power' \
  -Name 'PlatformAoAcOverride' -Value 0 -PropertyType DWord -Force
```

…via `tt/buw-jpS.PrivilegedSsh.sh bujn-winpc`?

content: We're trimming buw-hj0 to a top-level handbook and getting caparison-windows to land cleanly on rocket; preflight just caught a missing PlatformAoAcOverride registry value. Next: decide whether I set it via privileged SSH or you set it via regedit, then reboot rocket. (disable recaps in /config)

content.text:
## Two things going on

### 1. Yes, we're doing the operator-precondition facts twice

These three facts run at preflight (between phase 2 and phase 3), and again as facts 2/3/4 of post-completion `bujb_invigilate_windows`:

- registry `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion = 0`
- registry `PlatformAoAcOverride = 0`
- Tailscale service registered

That duplication is a side effect of how I wired the just-landed change: the helper is called from both call-sites, and `bujb_caparison_windows` invokes `bujb_invigilate_windows` post-completion which in turn calls the helper again. **Not a correctness bug** — those facts can't change between preflight and post-completion within a single caparison run. **Just wasted SSH round-trips** (~1–2 sec total). The defense-in-depth justification is real only for *standalone* invigilate runs (operator audits posture later); within a caparison run it's pure duplicate. We could add a `[caparison-internal]` flag, or have caparison call only the caparison-deliverable facts post-completion. Worth fixing but cheap to leave for now.

### 2. The terminal bug — invigilate is checking the wrong thing

The powercfg output on rocket:

```
The following sleep states are available on this system:
    Standby (S3)

The following sleep states are not available on this system:
    Standby (S1) ... S2 ... Hibernate ... S0 Low Power Idle ... Hybrid Sleep ... Fast Startup
```

**rocket is legacy-S3 hardware, not Modern Standby.** Hibernate is correctly off (caparison's `powercfg /h off` worked). S0 Modern Standby is "not available on this system" → **the entire AoAc override is moot on this hardware** — there's no S0 to override. Caparison's three powercfg calls (standby-timeout-ac/dc 0, hibernate-off) all succeeded silently.

What's failing: invigilate at line 1869–1881 parses powercfg output and dies if "Standby" or "Hibernate" appears in the *available* section. On rocket, **Standby (S3) is always available** because the firmware supports it. There is no powercfg command that removes S3 from the available list — that's a hardware-capability question, not a policy question. `powercfg -change standby-timeout-ac/dc 0` sets the *auto-sleep timeout* to "never," but the system **can still enter standby** (lid close, power button, programmatic suspend) and S3 stays "available."

The check conflates two different concepts:
- **Capability**: "can the system enter Standby?" (what `powercfg /a` reports — hardware)
- **Policy**: "will the system auto-enter Standby?" (what `powercfg /change standby-timeout-*` controls — what caparison actually sets)

Caparison enforces policy (timeout=0); the invigilate check verifies capability — different things. On Modern Standby hardware, AoAc override happens to remove S0 from "available" so the check coincidentally passes, which is probably how this assertion got past initial testing. On legacy-S3 hardware (rocket), the assertion is unsatisfiable.

The "or AoAc override missing" hint in the error message reinforces my read that the check was written with Modern Standby in mind — on legacy hardware the suggestion is a red herring.

**Right fix:** change the assertion to verify the *policy* caparison actually sets, not the capability. Something like `powercfg -query SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_SLEEP STANDBYIDLE` and assert `Current AC/DC Power Setting Index: 0x00000000`. Or drop the available-states check and rely on the timeout assertion.

Want me to fix the invigilate check (and the duplicate-work issue while I'm there)?

message.content: fix both

# bujb_invigilate_windows -- BUSJIW read-only host posture verification.
bujb_invigilate_windows() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_invigilate_windows: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
  zbujb_invigilate_assert_platform bubep_windows

  buc_step "Invigilate-windows: ${BUZ_FOLIO} (${BURN_HOST})"

  buc_step "  Fact: admin SSH reachable (key-only)"
  zbujb_admin_powershell 'exit 0' \
      > "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "admin SSH session unreachable under key-only auth (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"

  zbujb_invigilate_windows_op_facts

  buc_step "  Fact: Tailscale service StartType = Automatic"
  local z_start=""
  z_start=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged \
      "(Get-Service Tailscale).StartType") \
    || z_start="<unreadable>"
  test "${z_start}" = "Automatic" \
    || buc_die "Tailscale StartType: expected Automatic, got '${z_start:-<empty>}' — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"

  buc_step "  Fact: powercfg /a reports no Standby or Hibernate available"
  zbujb_admin_powershell 'powercfg /a' \
      > "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "powercfg /a failed on ${BURN_HOST} (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR})"
  local z_pcfg
  z_pcfg=$(<"${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}")
  z_pcfg="${z_pcfg//$'\r'/}"
  local z_available_section="${z_pcfg%%not available on this system*}"
  case "${z_available_section}" in
    *Standby*|*Hibernate*)
      buc_die "powercfg available sleep states: expected neither Standby nor Hibernate listed as available (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW) — or AoAc override missing"
      ;;
  esac

  buc_step "  Fact: WSL distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} registered"
  zbujb_admin_powershell 'wsl.exe --list --quiet' \
      > "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "wsl.exe --list --quiet failed on ${BURN_HOST} (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR})"
  local z_wsl
  z_wsl=$(<"${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}")
  z_wsl="${z_wsl//$'\r'/}"
  case $'\n'"${z_wsl}"$'\n' in
    *$'\n'"${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"$'\n'*) ;;
    *)
      buc_die "WSL distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}: expected present in wsl.exe --list --quiet, got '${z_wsl:-<none reported>}' — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
      ;;
  esac

  buc_step "Invigilate-windows succeeded"
}

input.new_string:
# zbujb_invigilate_windows_caparison_facts -- the four caparison-windows
# deliverables (Tailscale auto-start, sleep policy = never auto-sleep,
# hibernate disabled, WSL distribution registered). Shared between
# bujb_caparison_windows post-completion (verify our work landed without
# re-running the operator-precondition facts already checked at preflight)
# and bujb_invigilate_windows (full audit). Requires admin SSH established
# under key-only auth.
#
# The standby check intentionally verifies POLICY (standby-timeout 0)
# rather than CAPABILITY (powercfg /a "available" list). On legacy-S3
# hardware, S3 stays in the available list regardless of timeout policy
# because the firmware supports it; only AoAc-override on Modern Standby
# (S0) hardware coincidentally removes a state from "available". What
# caparison actually sets is the timeout, so that's what we verify.
zbujb_invigilate_windows_caparison_facts() {
  zbujb_sentinel

  buc_step "  Fact: Tailscale service StartType = Automatic"
  local z_start=""
  z_start=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged \
      "(Get-Service Tailscale).StartType") \
    || z_start="<unreadable>"
  test "${z_start}" = "Automatic" \
    || buc_die "Tailscale StartType: expected Automatic, got '${z_start:-<empty>}' — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"

  buc_step "  Fact: standby-timeout = 0 (AC and DC)"
  zbujb_admin_powershell 'powercfg /query SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_SLEEP STANDBYIDLE' \
      > "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "powercfg /query failed on ${BURN_HOST} (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR})"
  local z_pcfg
  z_pcfg=$(<"${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}")
  z_pcfg="${z_pcfg//$'\r'/}"
  case "${z_pcfg}" in
    *"Current AC Power Setting Index: 0x00000000"*) ;;
    *) buc_die "powercfg standby-timeout AC: expected 0x00000000 (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)" ;;
  esac
  case "${z_pcfg}" in
    *"Current DC Power Setting Index: 0x00000000"*) ;;
    *) buc_die "powercfg standby-timeout DC: expected 0x00000000 (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)" ;;
  esac

  buc_step "  Fact: hibernate disabled"
  zbujb_admin_powershell 'powercfg /a' \
      > "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "powercfg /a failed on ${BURN_HOST} (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR})"
  z_pcfg=$(<"${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}")
  z_pcfg="${z_pcfg//$'\r'/}"
  local z_available_section="${z_pcfg%%not available on this system*}"
  case "${z_available_section}" in
    *Hibernate*)
      buc_die "hibernate: expected absent from powercfg /a available section, got Hibernate listed as available (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
      ;;
  esac

  buc_step "  Fact: WSL distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} registered"
  zbujb_admin_powershell 'wsl.exe --list --quiet' \
      > "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "wsl.exe --list --quiet failed on ${BURN_HOST} (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR})"
  local z_wsl
  z_wsl=$(<"${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}")
  z_wsl="${z_wsl//$'\r'/}"
  case $'\n'"${z_wsl}"$'\n' in
    *$'\n'"${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"$'\n'*) ;;
    *)
      buc_die "WSL distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}: expected present in wsl.exe --list --quiet, got '${z_wsl:-<none reported>}' — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
      ;;
  esac
}

  local z_available_section="${z_pcfg%%not available on this system*}"
  case "${z_available_section}" in
    *Hibernate*)
      buc_die "hibernate: expected absent from powercfg /a available section, got Hibernate listed as available (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
      ;;
  esac

  buc_step "  Fact: WSL distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} registered"
  zbujb_admin_powershell 'wsl.exe --list --quiet' \
      > "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "wsl.exe --list --quiet failed on ${BURN_HOST} (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDERR})"
  local z_wsl
  z_wsl=$(<"${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}")
  z_wsl="${z_wsl//$'\r'/}"
  case $'\n'"${z_wsl}"$'\n' in
    *$'\n'"${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"$'\n'*) ;;
    *)
      buc_die "WSL distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}: expected present in wsl.exe --list --quiet, got '${z_wsl:-<none reported>}' — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
      ;;
  esac
}

# bujb_invigilate_windows -- BUSJIW read-only host posture verification.
bujb_invigilate_windows() {

lines: || buc_die "Tailscale service: expected non-empty Get-Service Tailscale, got <absent> — operator handbook BUSJHW (install + Run-Unattended + first auth)"
+# deliverables (Tailscale auto-start, sleep policy = never auto-sleep,
+# hibernate disabled, WSL distribution registered). Shared between
+# bujb_caparison_windows post-completion (verify our work landed without
+# re-running the operator-precondition facts already checked at preflight)
+# and bujb_invigilate_windows (full audit). Requires admin SSH established
+# The standby check intentionally verifies POLICY (standby-timeout 0)
+# rather than CAPABILITY (powercfg /a "available" list). On legacy-S3
+# hardware, S3 stays in the available list regardless of timeout policy
+# because the firmware supports it; only AoAc-override on Modern Standby
+# (S0) hardware coincidentally removes a state from "available". What
+# caparison actually sets is the timeout, so that's what we verify.
+  buc_step "  Fact: Tailscale service StartType = Automatic"
+    || buc_die "Tailscale StartType: expected Automatic, got '${z_start:-<empty>}' — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
+  local z_available_section="${z_pcfg%%not available on this system*}"
+      buc_die "hibernate: expected absent from powercfg /a available section, got Hibernate listed as available (see ${ZBUJB_INVIGILATE_STDOUT}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
+      buc_die "WSL distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}: expected present in wsl.exe --list --quiet, got '${z_wsl:-<none reported>}' — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
# bujb_invigilate_windows -- BUSJIW read-only host posture verification.

input.params: {"identity": "A-", "files": ["Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh"], "intent": "Two fixes in bujb_jurisdiction.sh. (1) Eliminate duplicate operator-precondition fact checks during caparison-windows: extracted the four caparison-deliverable facts (Tailscale StartType, standby-timeout policy, hibernate disabled, WSL distribution registered) into a new private helper zbujb_invigilate_windows_caparison_facts, parallel to the existing zbujb_invigilate_windows_op_facts. bujb_invigilate_windows now calls both helpers (op + caparison) for full audit; bujb_caparison_windows now calls only zbujb_invigilate_windows_caparison_facts post-completion (operator preconditions already verified at preflight between phase 2 and phase 3, can't change mid-run; admin SSH already verified by phase 2). Net: caparison's happy-path SSH round-trips reduced by 4 (3 op-facts + admin SSH check); full standalone invigilate-windows behavior unchanged. (2) Fix the powercfg-available semantics bug that made caparison unsatisfiable on legacy-S3 hardware. The original check parsed powercfg /a output for 'Standby' or 'Hibernate' in the available section and dies if either appears — but on legacy hardware that supports S3 (firmware-level Standby), S3 ALWAYS appears in 'available' regardless of powercfg timeout policy. The check conflated capability ('can the system enter Standby?' — hardware feature) with policy ('will the system auto-enter Standby?' — what caparison actually controls via powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac/dc 0). Replaced with a policy-shaped check that queries powercfg /query SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_SLEEP STANDBYIDLE and asserts both 'Current AC Power Setting Index: 0x00000000' and 'Current DC Power Setting Index: 0x00000000' substrings. Hibernate check kept as a powercfg /a available-section probe since powercfg /h off DOES correctly remove Hibernate from the available list; just narrowed the case pattern from *Standby*|*Hibernate* to *Hibernate* so legacy-S3 Standby presence no longer trips it. Inline comment in the new helper documents the capability-vs-policy distinction so the next reader understands why we deliberately don't use powercfg /a for the Standby check. The 'or AoAc override missing' red-herring suffix in the original error string is gone — that hint was misleading on legacy-S3 hardware where AoAc override (Modern Standby S0 suppression) is moot. Gates: bash -n on bujb_jurisdiction.sh; tt/buw-st 5 fixtures / 28 cases; tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.handbook-render.sh 15/15; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 4 fixtures / 98 cases. Field exercise on rocket: deferred — operator can re-run tt/buw-jpCW.CaparisonWindows.sh bujn-winpc to verify both fixes (no duplicate facts in trace; powercfg standby-timeout policy passes regardless of S3 hardware capability). Heat-affiliated commit (no pace) per the operator's outside-of-paces direction."}

content.text: 9153ea9612d3d1ae7832d7ca7cbd0cc2d96cc7e4

Notched as `9153ea96` (heat ₣A-).

Two fixes in one commit:

1. **Duplicate work eliminated** — new `zbujb_invigilate_windows_caparison_facts` helper (parallel to `_op_facts`). `bujb_invigilate_windows` calls both for full audit; `bujb_caparison_windows` post-completion calls only the deliverables helper. Saves 4 SSH round-trips per caparison run (3 op-facts + redundant admin-SSH check).

2. **Powercfg semantics bug fixed** — Standby check now queries policy (`powercfg /query SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_SLEEP STANDBYIDLE` → `Current AC/DC Power Setting Index: 0x00000000`) instead of capability (`powercfg /a` available-states list, which always shows S3 on legacy hardware). Hibernate check narrowed to just `*Hibernate*` since `powercfg /h off` does correctly remove Hibernate from the available list. Inline comment in the helper explains the capability-vs-policy distinction so the next reader doesn't relapse.

Re-run `tt/buw-jpCW.CaparisonWindows.sh bujn-winpc` to verify both: trace should show no duplicate op-facts, and the standby check should pass on rocket regardless of S3 hardware capability.

{bust_interactive_session}::
`buw-jws` —
Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.

==== Handbook

[[bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top}::
`buw-hj0` —
Top-level handbook landing for jurisdiction procedures; dispatches
to the per-platform handbooks below.

[[bust_handbook_windows]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_windows}::
`buw-hjW` —
Per-platform handbook for Windows operator-manual scope: OpenSSH
server install + reachability, security-sensitive registry edits,
Tailscale install + Run-Unattended + first auth, netplwiz
auto-login.

[[bust_handbook_macos]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_macos}::
`buw-hjM` —
Per-platform handbook for macOS operator-manual scope: Tailscale
install + login, Privacy & Security consent prompts.

[[bust_handbook_linux]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_linux}::
`buw-hjL` —
Per-platform handbook for Linux operator-manual scope: Tailscale
install + login, sshd install/enable on minimal distros.

=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_caparison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_caparison}::
Establish admin host posture per platform — SSH key trust plus the
host-side state (sshd_config hardening on Windows OpenSSH, WSL
distribution staging, power/sleep configuration, Tailscale service
auto-start where applicable) — leaving the host ready for
{busn_garrison} and {busn_invigilate} verification.
Per-platform tabtargets
(`buw-jpCW`, `buw-jpCM`, `buw-jpCL`);
each implements its platform's posture establishment from a single
operator-facing verb.

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_invigilate_windows}
3	
4	NOTE: {busn_invigilate} on Windows -- read-only host posture
5	verification. Asserts each posture fact matches expected; no
6	mutations.
7	
8	NOTE: Invocation: {bust_caparison_windows} post-completion check;
9	{bust_garrison_cygwin} and {bust_garrison_wsl} precondition.
10	
11	NOTE: Failure pointers discriminate operator-managed
12	({bust_handbook_windows}) from caparison-managed
13	({bust_caparison_windows}). PowerShell reads via
14	`Get-ItemPropertyValue`, `Get-Service`, `powercfg /a`, `wsl.exe --list
15	--quiet`; bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracts each fact.
16	
17	The {busn_invigilate} operation on Windows:
18	
19	//axhopt_typed_parameter

21	
22	//axhoq_precondition
23	A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
24	{burn_regime} targets a Windows OpenSSH node reachable as
25	{burp_privileged_user} under key-only auth using
26	{burp_privileged_key_file}. Admin SSH failure surfaces as the first
27	posture fact -- invigilate presupposes trust, does not establish it.
28	
29	//axhos_step
30	. *Open admin SSH session*:
31	.. {busc_call}
32	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with {burp_privileged_key_file}; key-auth only
33	.. {busc_store}
34	`«ADMIN_SESSION»`
35	.. {busc_fatal}
36	on connection failure -- {bust_caparison_windows} scope
37	
38	//axhos_step
39	. *Assert operator-managed registry keys*:
40	.. For each {bust_handbook_windows}-established registry value
41	(canonical: `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` under
42	`HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device`):
43	... {busc_call}
44	PowerShell `Get-ItemPropertyValue` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
45	... {busc_store}
46	`«REG_VALUE»`
47	... {busc_require}
48	`«REG_VALUE»` matches expected
49	... {busc_fatal}
50	on mismatch -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope
51	
52	//axhos_step
53	. *Assert AoAc override*:

55	PowerShell `Get-ItemPropertyValue` for

57	.. {busc_store}
58	`«REG_AOAC»`
59	.. {busc_require}

63	
64	//axhos_step

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_caparison_windows}
3	
4	NOTE: {busn_caparison} on Windows -- three phases. Phase 1: admin SSH
5	key trust + `sshd_config` hardening. Phase 2: key-only reconnect.
6	Phase 3: post-trust admin posture (WSL distribution, powercfg,
7	Tailscale service). Closes with {bust_invigilate_windows}.
8	
9	NOTE: Nuclear -- every invocation purges and re-establishes the
10	caparison-managed posture; no skip-if-present logic.
11	{bust_handbook_windows} owns operator-manual posture; caparison owns
12	the idempotent admin-shell remainder.
13	
14	NOTE: Phase 1's `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own ssh session
15	-- the post-restart disconnect is the expected phase boundary, not a
16	failure. PowerShell-side operations use `Get-Content`, `Set-Service`,
17	`Start-Service`, `Restart-Service`, `wsl.exe --import`; bash-side
18	BCG-compliant matching extracts effective `sshd_config` directives
19	from the raw bytes.
20	
21	The {busn_caparison} operation on Windows:
22	
23	//axhopt_typed_parameter
24	{burp_investiture}
25	
26	//axhoq_precondition
27	A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
28	{burn_regime} targets a Windows OpenSSH node.
29	
30	//axhoq_precondition
31	{bust_handbook_windows} operator-manual scope is complete -- OpenSSH
32	Server installed and reachable, security-sensitive registry edits
33	applied (`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion`, `PlatformAoAcOverride`),
34	Tailscale installed + Run-Unattended + first auth, netplwiz
35	auto-login, and {burp_privileged_user} has a known password.
36	Caparison presupposes this scope.
37	
38	//axhoq_precondition
39	{burp_privileged_user} is in the Administrators group;
40	{burp_privileged_key_file} is unencrypted.
41	
42	//axhos_step
43	. *Phase 1 — open admin SSH session*:
44	.. {busc_call}
45	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
46	`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` (steady-state: key via
47	{burp_privileged_key_file}; first run: TTY password)
48	.. {busc_store}
49	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` for phase 1 operations
50	.. {busc_fatal}
51	on connection failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope
52	
53	//axhos_step
54	. *Phase 1 — derive admin pubkey*:
55	.. {busc_call}
56	`ssh-keygen -y` on {burp_privileged_key_file}
57	.. {busc_store}
58	`«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` from stdout
59	.. {busc_fatal}
60	on derivation failure -- privkey encrypted (unsupported) or unreadable
61	
62	//axhos_step
63	. *Phase 1 — install admin pubkey*:
64	.. write `«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` to `administrators_authorized_keys` over
65	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` as a bare key entry (no `command=`)
66	.. nuclear -- file replaced, not merged
67	.. {busc_fatal}
68	on write failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope
69	
70	//axhos_step
71	. *Phase 1 — lock down administrators_authorized_keys ACL*:
72	.. {busc_call}
73	`icacls` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- ACL restricted to SYSTEM and
74	Administrators (Windows OpenSSH refuses the file otherwise);
75	idempotent
76	.. {busc_fatal}
77	on `icacls` failure -- phase 2 reconnect would fail
78	
79	//axhos_step
80	. *Phase 1 — write hardened sshd_config directives*:
81	.. write `PubkeyAuthentication yes`, `PasswordAuthentication no`,
82	`PermitEmptyPasswords no` to `sshd_config` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»`;
83	idempotent
84	.. {busc_fatal}
85	on write failure -- restart would still serve password auth
86	
87	//axhos_step
88	. *Phase 1 — verify hardened directives took*:
89	.. {busc_call}
90	PowerShell `Get-Content` on `sshd_config` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»`
91	.. {busc_store}
92	`«SSHD_CONFIG_BYTES»`
93	.. {busc_require}
94	`«SSHD_CONFIG_BYTES»` parses to `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
95	`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` as effective
96	directive values (BCG-compliant matching)
97	.. {busc_fatal}
98	on mismatch -- prior write did not take or stale override shadows
99	
100	//axhos_step
101	. *Phase 1 — validate sshd_config*:
102	.. {busc_call}
103	`sshd -t` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- penultimate atomic op of phase 1
104	.. {busc_store}
105	`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»`
106	.. {busc_require}
107	`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»` is zero
108	.. {busc_fatal}
109	on non-zero -- aborting preserves the running sshd
110	
111	//axhos_step
112	. *Phase 1 — restart sshd*:
113	.. {busc_call}
114	`Restart-Service sshd` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- final atomic op
115	of phase 1
116	.. {busc_fatal}
117	on invocation failure (the post-restart disconnect itself is the
118	expected phase boundary)
119	
120	//axhos_step
121	. *Phase 2 — reconnect under key-only auth*:
122	.. {busc_call}
123	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
124	`PreferredAuthentications=publickey` using {burp_privileged_key_file}
125	.. {busc_store}
126	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` for phase 3 operations
127	.. {busc_fatal}
128	on reconnect failure -- brick (manual password no longer opens a
129	session); recover via operator's local console
130	
131	//axhos_step
132	. *Phase 3 — stage rbtww-main WSL distribution*:
133	.. {busc_call}
134	`wsl.exe --import` from the Ubuntu-24.04 seed over
135	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` to register `rbtww-main` under the admin user
136	(canonical: `BUJB_wsl_distribution` in {bujb_jurisdiction})
137	.. nuclear -- always purge-and-reimport
138	.. {busc_fatal}
139	on import failure -- WSL feature disabled, seed unavailable, or
140	admin user's HKCU\\Lxss state corrupted
141	
142	//axhos_step
143	. *Phase 3 — disable powercfg sleep and hibernate*:
144	.. {busc_call}
145	`powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0`,
146	`powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0`,
147	`powercfg /hibernate off` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
148	.. {busc_fatal}
149	on `powercfg` failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (Modern
150	Standby AoAc override demotes calls to no-ops)
151	
152	//axhos_step
153	. *Phase 3 — set Tailscale service Automatic and running*:
154	.. {busc_call}
155	`Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic` then
156	`Start-Service -Name Tailscale` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
157	.. {busc_fatal}
158	on `Set-Service` or `Start-Service` failure --
159	{bust_handbook_windows} scope (install + Run-Unattended + first auth)
160	
161	//axhos_step
162	. *Post-completion check — run invigilate-windows*:
163	.. {busc_call}
164	{bust_invigilate_windows} for this {burp_investiture}
165	.. {busc_store}
166	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»`
167	.. {busc_require}
168	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»` is zero
169	.. {busc_fatal}
170	on non-zero -- invigilate diagnostic surfaced verbatim
171	
172	//axhog_guarantee
173	On success:
174	
175	* Admin SSH is key-only with admin pubkey installed in
176	  `administrators_authorized_keys` under correct ACLs.
177	* `sshd_config` carries `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
178	  `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`.
179	* Post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.
180	* `rbtww-main` WSL distribution is registered under the admin user.
181	* `powercfg` sleep and hibernate are disabled.
182	* Tailscale service is Automatic and running.
183	* {bust_invigilate_windows} confirms every posture fact end-to-end.
184	
185	//axhoc_completion
186	Exit 0 when the post-completion {bust_invigilate_windows} passes.
187	Non-zero on any phase-1 op failure (admin SSH, pubkey, ACL,
188	`sshd_config`, `sshd -t`), phase-2 reconnect failure, any phase-3 op
189	failure (`wsl --import`, `powercfg`, Tailscale), or post-completion
190	invigilate failure.
191

content.text:
Yes — meaningful spec drift in three subdocs and one consequential **name mismatch**. Here's the audit:

## 1. Name collision — pick a side

**BUS0 line 1946** predicts `buw-hjW` (capital W) for the Windows handbook subprocedure:

```
[[bust_handbook_windows]]
{bust_handbook_windows}::
`buw-hjW` —
Per-platform handbook for Windows operator-manual scope: OpenSSH
server install + reachability, security-sensitive registry edits,
Tailscale install + Run-Unattended + first auth, netplwiz auto-login.
```

But we created `buw-hjw` (lowercase). The capital-letter convention here matches the rest of BUS0 — `buw-jpCW`, `buw-jpCM`, `buw-jpCL` for caparison platforms; spec also predicts `buw-hjM` and `buw-hjL` for Mac/Linux. **The spec convention is correct** — capital letters identify platforms (W/M/L); lowercase letters identify shell types (b/c/w). Recommend renaming our tabtarget to `buw-hjW`.

## 2. BUSJIW (Invigilate Windows spec) — same bugs we just fixed in code

- **Line 41-42**: spec carries the same `Windows\CurrentVersion` path bug (missing `NT`) we fixed in code. Also infectious in **BUSJHW line 96** — handbook spec has the same wrong `reg add` command.
- **Lines 87-95**: spec says "powercfg /a reports neither standby nor hibernate as available" — exactly the buggy capability-vs-policy conflation we fixed in code. Spec needs the same split: standby-timeout policy + hibernate disabled.
- **Line 95**: "or AoAc override missing" misleading hint — spec needs the same drop.

## 3. BUSJCW (Caparison Windows spec) — drift from new orchestration

- **Missing**: the new preflight gate between phase 2 and phase 3 that asserts operator-preconditions before the long WSL stage.
- **Lines 161-170**: "Post-completion check — run invigilate-windows" — caparison now calls only the deliverables subset (`zbujb_invigilate_windows_caparison_facts`), not full invigilate. The spec wording "run invigilate-windows" is now inaccurate; should be "verify caparison deliverables (the invigilate fact subset that caparison itself produces)."

## 4. BUS0 line 2016 — same drift as BUSJCW

References "caparison's post-completion check (caparison runs invigilate post-completion)" — now nuanced.

## Recommended sequence

1. **Rename** `tt/buw-hjw.HandbookJurisdictionWindows.sh` → `tt/buw-hjW.HandbookJurisdictionWindows.sh` (and the BUWZ_HJW_WINDOWS enrollment colophon string). One-line rename + grep sweep. Keeps spec/code aligned with the unwritten platform-letter convention. **Do this even if no spec edits — wrong-case colophons accumulate.**

2. **Fix the registry-path bug** in BUSJIW line 41-42 + BUSJHW line 96 — single character insert (`Windows` → `Windows NT`). Defensive: add a `:bubc_windows_passwordless_path:` sort of attribute reference if the spec uses linked terms for paths, or just hardcode (the bash side now has the constant; spec is human-readable).

3. **Update BUSJIW step 6** (powercfg) — split into "standby-timeout policy = 0" (verify caparison's `powercfg /change`) and "hibernate disabled" (verify `powercfg /h off` via available-states absence). Drop the "or AoAc override missing" tail.

4. **Add a BUSJCW preflight step** between current phase 2 and phase 3 that calls `bust_invigilate_windows`'s operator-precondition fact subset. Update the post-completion step wording to reflect deliverables-only check.

Want me to do all four? They're interrelated — easier as one sweep than separately. Heat-affiliated commit, or do you want to scope this as a pace given that it's spec work?

file.content:
[[busn_invigilate]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_invigilate}::
Verify admin host posture per platform, read-only — assert each
posture fact (admin SSH key trust, service states, registry values,
power configuration, host-availability resources) matches its
expected value, with no mutations and no remediation.
Per-platform tabtargets
(`buw-jpIW`, `buw-jpIM`, `buw-jpIL`);
each owns its platform's posture fact list.
Failure pointers discriminate operator-managed posture
(→ the per-platform handbook) from caparison-managed posture
(→ the per-platform caparison).
+
Used twice: as
{busn_caparison}'s post-completion check (caparison runs invigilate
after applying its operations to verify they took), and as
{busn_garrison}'s precondition check (garrison runs invigilate
before any destructive workload work).

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Provision or refresh the workload account on a node from an existing
admin SSH foothold.
A single tabtarget per shell-letter
(`buw-jpGb`, `buw-jpGc`, `buw-jpGw`)
runs the same destructive ceremony with the shell-letter selecting
remote shell mappings via
{bujb_jurisdiction}.
Garrison-`b` is the native-bash workload-shell flavor on both Linux
and macOS; step bodies branch on {burn_platform}.
The elevation matrix differs across letters:
garrison-`b` runs destructive ops via `sudo -n` (Linux + Mac);

input.old_string:
phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session) places the
admin pubkey, applies icacls lockdown, writes hardened sshd_config
directives, validates via `sshd -t`, and restarts sshd; phase 2
(key-auth-only reconnect) verifies the post-restart admin session
authenticates by key alone. Post-trust admin posture (WSL stage,
powercfg, Tailscale service) runs after phase 2. The bash
orchestrator treats the disconnect immediately after
`Restart-Service sshd` as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.

the expected phase boundary, not a failure.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
//axhob_operation
{bust_handbook_windows}

NOTE: Operator-manual scope for Windows -- posture {busn_caparison}
cannot or should not automate. No remote contact, no profile mutation.

NOTE: Steps establish OpenSSH server reachability,
security-sensitive registry edits, Tailscale install + Run-Unattended
+ first auth, and netplwiz auto-login. Mismatches surface from
{bust_invigilate_windows} as failure pointers back to this handbook.

NOTE: Steps missing here cause {bust_caparison_windows} to fail at
the point that depends on them.

The {bust_handbook_windows} procedure:

//axhos_step
. *Install OpenSSH Server*:
.. {bush_show}
GUI path: Settings → Apps → Optional features → Add a feature →
search "OpenSSH Server" → Install
.. Elevated PowerShell equivalent:
+
----
Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0
----
.. {bush_require}
`Get-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0`
reports `Installed`

//axhos_step
. *Set sshd to Automatic and start*: from an elevated PowerShell
prompt:
+
----
Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic
Start-Service -Name sshd
----
.. {bush_require}
`Get-Service sshd` reports `Status` as `Running`

//axhos_step
. *Open the SSH port through Windows Firewall*: from an elevated
PowerShell prompt:
+
----
New-NetFirewallRule -Name sshd -DisplayName 'OpenSSH Server (sshd)' -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort 22
----
.. {bush_show}
substitute the non-default port if the BURN profile carries one
.. {bush_show}
recent Windows builds create this rule automatically during the
OpenSSH install -- this step is a verify-and-add fallback
.. {bush_require}
`Get-NetFirewallRule -Name sshd` returns the rule

//axhos_step
. *Enable temporary password authentication*:
.. open `C:\ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config` in an editor running as
Administrator
.. set `PasswordAuthentication yes`, save
.. restart the service:
+
----
Restart-Service sshd
----
.. {bush_show}
intentionally temporary -- {bust_caparison_windows} flips it back
to `no` during phase 1; this step makes the first-run TTY password
fall-through possible before any key is installed

//axhos_step
. *Ensure the admin user has a known password*:
.. {bush_require}
{burp_privileged_user} is a member of the Administrators group with
a password the operator knows
.. set or verify via `lusrmgr.msc` (GUI) or from an elevated cmd
prompt:
+
----
net user <username> <newpassword>
----
.. {bush_show}
required for caparison's phase-1 password fallback -- operator types
it once on `/dev/tty`; caparison itself never handles the password

//axhos_step
. *Apply security-sensitive registry edits*:
.. {bush_warn}
each entry requires elevation -- use `reg add` from an elevated cmd
prompt or `New-ItemProperty` from an elevated PowerShell prompt
.. `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` = `0` (DWORD) under
`HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device`:
+
----
reg add "HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device" /v DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f
----
.. {bush_show}
exposes the netplwiz "Users must enter a user name and password"
checkbox on Windows 10/11
.. `PlatformAoAcOverride` = `0` (DWORD) under
`HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power`:
+
----
reg add "HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power" /v PlatformAoAcOverride /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f
----
.. {bush_show}
required on Modern Standby (AlwaysOnAlwaysConnected) systems --
without it, caparison's `powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0` is
silently demoted to a no-op
.. {bush_require}
both keys present -- asserted by {bust_invigilate_windows}

//axhos_step
. *Install Tailscale*:
.. download the Tailscale for Windows MSI from
https://tailscale.com/download
.. run the installer
.. {bush_require}
Windows service named `Tailscale` is registered and a system-tray
icon is present

//axhos_step
. *Toggle Tailscale Run-Unattended*:
.. open the Tailscale system-tray icon → Preferences → enable "Run
unattended"
.. {bush_show}
required because the host runs without a console login during
automated tests -- {bust_caparison_windows} sets the service
`StartupType` to `Automatic`, but useful traffic only flows once
Run-Unattended is on

//axhos_step
. *Authenticate Tailscale to the operator's tailnet*:
.. open the Tailscale system-tray icon → Sign in
.. complete the browser-based first-time authentication
.. {bush_show}
binds the host to the tailnet so the BURN profile's tailnet
hostname (or `100.x.y.z` address) becomes a stable transport
address that persists across reboots

//axhos_step
. *Configure netplwiz auto-login*:
.. {bush_warn}
depends on `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` from the registry step
above -- without it the checkbox is not visible
.. press Windows + R, type `netplwiz`, press Enter
.. select {burp_privileged_user} in the User Accounts dialog
.. uncheck "Users must enter a user name and password to use this
computer"
.. click Apply, then enter the admin password twice when prompted
.. {bush_show}
host now boots straight to a logged-in admin desktop -- the
substrate Tailscale Run-Unattended and OpenSSH depend on for
unattended operation

//axhog_guarantee
On completion:

* No remote contact occurred and no local file was written by the
  handbook itself.
* OpenSSH Server is installed, reachable, and reachable through the
  firewall with temporary password auth enabled.
* {burp_privileged_user} has a known password and Administrators
  membership.
* `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` and `PlatformAoAcOverride`
  registry values are set.
* Tailscale is installed, Run-Unattended is on, and the host is
  authenticated to the operator's tailnet.
* netplwiz auto-login is configured for {burp_privileged_user}.
* The operator-managed posture scope that {bust_caparison_windows}
  and {bust_invigilate_windows} depend on is established.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 after handbook content is printed. Non-zero only when content
cannot be located or rendered.

prompt or `New-ItemProperty` from an elevated PowerShell prompt

input.params: {"identity": "A-", "files": ["Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc", "Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJIW-InvigilateWindows.adoc", "Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJHW-HandbookWindows.adoc", "Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJCW-CaparisonWindows.adoc"], "intent": "Align BUS0 + jurisdiction subdocs (BUSJIW, BUSJHW, BUSJCW) to the four code changes that landed in the recent heat-affiliated commits. (1) BUS0 lowercase rename: bust_handbook_windows tabtarget colophon was predicted as buw-hjW (capital W); operator preference is lowercase, so spec now reads buw-hjw. Lowercased bust_handbook_macos (buw-hjm) and bust_handbook_linux (buw-hjl) for symmetry — these are predictions for as-yet-unbuilt platform handbook subprocedures, so the rename is free; the lowercase convention now applies uniformly across the bust_handbook_* family even though caparison and garrison families retain capital platform letters (buw-jpCW, buw-jpCM, buw-jpCL). (2) BUSJIW + BUSJHW registry-path bug: spec carried the same Windows\\CurrentVersion path that was wrong in code (missing 'NT' segment under HKLM\\Software\\Microsoft); both updated to Windows NT\\CurrentVersion in the canonical-path callout (BUSJIW step 'Assert operator-managed registry keys') and in the reg add command example (BUSJHW step 'Apply security-sensitive registry edits'). (3) BUSJIW powercfg semantics: replaced the single 'Assert powercfg sleep/hibernate disabled' step (asserted 'powercfg /a reports neither standby nor hibernate as available' — capability-vs-policy conflation that is unsatisfiable on legacy-S3 firmware) with two policy-shaped steps. New 'Assert standby-timeout policy = 0 (AC and DC)' step calls 'powercfg /query SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_SLEEP STANDBYIDLE' and requires both 'Current AC/DC Power Setting Index: 0x00000000' substrings; inline rationale documents the capability-vs-policy distinction so the next reader does not relapse. New 'Assert hibernate disabled' step still uses 'powercfg /a' but narrows the assertion to 'Hibernate not in available section' (powercfg /h off does correctly remove Hibernate from the available list, so this is the right shape for hibernate). The misleading 'or AoAc override missing' fatal hint was dropped — that suggestion was Modern-Standby-S0-specific and a red herring on legacy-S3 hardware where AoAc override is moot. (4) BUSJCW preflight gate: inserted a new step between Phase 2 ('reconnect under key-only auth') and Phase 3 ('stage rbtww-main WSL distribution') named 'Preflight — verify operator-precondition facts before phase 3'. The step calls bust_invigilate_windows's operator-precondition fact subset (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion, PlatformAoAcOverride, Tailscale-service-registered) over the just-established phase-2 ADMIN_SESSION_P2 and fails fast on missing operator scope, with rationale that the gate prevents wasting the multi-minute WSL download cycle on a host whose operator scope is incomplete. Updated the post-completion step from 'run invigilate-windows' (full) to 'verify caparison deliverables' (the four caparison-deliverable facts only) — the wording, the store-name, and the fatal pointer all now reflect that operator-precondition facts already verified at preflight do not re-run within a single caparison run, and admin SSH is not re-probed because phase 2 just established it. Guarantee section's invigilate bullet split: post-completion uses the deliverables subset; standalone invigilate run remains the way to assert the full fact list end-to-end. Completion clause's preflight failure mode added; post-completion failure mode renamed deliverables-subset to match step wording. (5) BUS0 prose updates: caparison's 'Post-trust admin posture (WSL stage, powercfg, Tailscale service) runs after phase 2' sentence reframed to acknowledge the preflight gate ('Between phase 2 and the post-trust admin posture, caparison-windows runs a preflight gate that asserts invigilate's operator-precondition fact subset...'). The busn_invigilate 'Used twice' clause expanded to 'Used three times in the caparison/garrison flow' enumerating preflight + post-completion (deliverables only) + garrison precondition (full); standalone invocation runs the full fact list. Spec drift now closed against the 9153ea96 + 83419627 + 56aef71e bash work. Gates: tt/buw-st 5 fixtures / 28 cases; tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.handbook-render.sh 15/15; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 4 fixtures / 98 cases. No code changes in this notch — spec-only sweep against the just-landed bash. Heat-affiliated commit (no pace) per the operator's outside-of-paces direction."}

content.text:
Yes — and the trace tells a specific story worth naming.

## What landed cleanly

- **Preflight gate fired correctly**: 3 op-facts in seconds (Phase 2 → Preflight → Phase 3), no wasted ~5min WSL download cycle on missing operator state.
- **Post-completion is exactly 4 deliverables**: Tailscale StartType, standby-timeout, hibernate, WSL — no duplicate op-facts, no redundant admin-SSH probe. The decomposition into `_op_facts` + `_caparison_facts` paid off as designed.
- **Powercfg policy check passed on legacy-S3 hardware** (rocket): `standby-timeout = 0 (AC and DC)` succeeded where the old `powercfg /a` available-states assertion was structurally unsatisfiable.
- **Caparison-windows succeeded** — actual end-to-end on a real Windows host.

## What concerns me about how we got here

This is a small-scale study in **spec-as-documentation rather than spec-as-contract**:

1. **BUSJIW shipped with two conceptual bugs**: registry path typo (`Windows` vs `Windows NT`) and capability-vs-policy conflation. The bash code mirrored both. The spec didn't catch the bash, the bash didn't catch the spec — they were written in lockstep with the same blind spots.
2. **No test exercises invigilate against a live host**. The `handbook-render` fixture catches whether prose renders; nothing catches whether asserted PowerShell commands actually return what the spec claims they return on real hardware. Both bugs would have surfaced on first contact with rocket — and did, just years later than they could have.
3. **The handbook was missing a real precondition** (AoAc registry step). Operator discovered it the hard way; we backfilled it. Preventable if the handbook was generated from (or at least cross-checked against) the same fact list invigilate enforces.
4. **The constants we minted prevent future path drift structurally** — that's the durable win from this session. `BUBC_windows_passwordless_path` is now the single source of truth; handbook prose and invigilate query both source from it. The next time someone "fixes" one site, the other doesn't silently rot.

## Net read

The work succeeded as a tactical fix and produced a structural improvement (shared constants, decomposed invigilate, preflight gate, fixed semantics). The fact that it took **5 commits** in one session to land what is conceptually a small change is the diagnostic — the underlying issue is the absence of a feedback loop that would have caught this on the first invigilate write. That's a future investment, not something to fix now: a `tt/buw-jpIW.InvigilateWindows.sh` exercise routinely run against a known-good host would prevent the next class-of-bug.

Final state is clean and the spec is aligned. Worth notching the run as a known-good baseline if you want a reference point in git for "first end-to-end caparison-windows on rocket."

content.text:
No structural blocker from our recent work — caparison-windows is the foundation garrison's precondition check verifies, and that's clean now. A few things worth knowing before you pull the trigger:

**1. Garrison-w specifically is field-untested.** The most recent garrison-w refactor (commit `6b505a5f`, "garrison-w refactor: workload owns its WSL distribution") explicitly deferred end-to-end Windows verification: *"Empirical end-to-end deferred to the next pace per docket."* The three-namespace shape (Windows account + per-user HKCU\Lxss distribution + WSL-Linux user inside the workload-owned distribution) and the SSH-as-workload conducted-by-orchestrator approach haven't actually been exercised against a real Windows node yet. So garrison-w may surface integration bugs the same way invigilate just did.

**2. Garrison is "nuclear" per spec.** Every invocation purges and re-establishes the workload state — no skip-if-present logic. If rocket has any prior `bujn-winpc` workload artifacts (workload user account, WSL distribution under workload's HKCU, key in `authorized_keys`), they get wiped first. Idempotent, but worth knowing.

**3. Garrison's invigilate-precondition will re-run all 7 facts.** Spec says garrison calls the full invigilate (not the caparison-deliverable subset). That's deliberate — garrison wants to verify operator scope hasn't drifted since caparison ran. Should pass cleanly given we just landed; if it doesn't, fail-fast before destructive work.

**4. Likely needs `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE` in place.** Garrison plants a workload key in the workload account's `authorized_keys`. Operator owns the key material; if the BURP profile points at a path that doesn't exist or holds an encrypted key, garrison fails at the plant step.

**Recommendation**: try `buw-jpGc` (Cygwin) first if you want to exercise garrison's general path with the simpler shell mapping — that's been more recently exercised. Try `buw-jpGw` (WSL) if you specifically want to validate the rbtww-main seed → workload-HKCU import flow that just landed.

Which variant are you aiming for?
